input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML – use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=Black, Dark=White}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=Black}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework watches `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` and swaps values automatically when the OS theme changes—no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent – `SetAppThemeColor` / `SetAppTheme`:**

```csharp
// For Color-typed properties (shorthand helper)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);

// For any property type (generic version)
myPage.SetAppTheme<Color>(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

myButton.SetAppTheme<Color>(
    Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Trigger | Responds to OS-level theme change in real time |
| Fallback | If `Unspecified`, the `Light` value is used |
| Scope | Works on any bindable property, not just colors |
| Alternative | For larger apps, consider resource dictionaries with `AppThemeBinding` on merged styles instead of per-element bindings |
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about .NET MAUI's `AppThemeBinding` markup extension for automatic light/dark theme switching. This is a knowledge/explanation question, not a code change task. I'll provide a concise, practical answer with XAML and C# examples.
